name: AWS Access Key
description: AWS access key ID detected. These keys grant programmatic access to AWS services.
recommendation: Remove the key from source code immediately. Rotate the key in AWS IAM console. Use environment variables or AWS credential files instead.

name: AWS Secret Key
description: AWS secret access key detected in code.
recommendation: Remove immediately. Rotate in AWS IAM. Use environment variables or IAM roles.

name: OpenAI API Key
description: OpenAI API key detected. This key provides access to OpenAI services and may incur costs.
recommendation: Remove the key from source code. Rotate it in the OpenAI dashboard. Use environment variables.

name: OpenAI Project Key
description: OpenAI project-scoped API key detected.
recommendation: Remove from source code and use environment variables. Rotate the key in OpenAI settings.

name: Anthropic API Key
description: Anthropic API key detected. This key provides access to Claude and other Anthropic services.
recommendation: Remove from source code. Rotate in Anthropic console. Use environment variables.

name: GitHub Personal Access Token
description: GitHub personal access token detected. Can grant access to repositories and GitHub APIs.
recommendation: Remove from source code. Revoke and regenerate in GitHub Settings > Developer settings.

name: GitHub OAuth Token
description: GitHub OAuth access token detected.
recommendation: Remove from source code. Revoke the OAuth app token in GitHub settings.

name: GitHub User-to-Server Token
description: GitHub user-to-server installation token detected.
recommendation: Remove from source code. These tokens should only be generated at runtime.

name: GitHub Server-to-Server Token
description: GitHub server-to-server installation token detected.
recommendation: Remove from source code. Generate these tokens dynamically via the GitHub App API.

name: GitHub Refresh Token
description: GitHub refresh token detected.
recommendation: Remove from source code. Store securely and refresh tokens at runtime.

name: Stripe Secret Key
description: Stripe live secret key detected. This key can process real payments and access customer data.
recommendation: Remove immediately. Rotate in Stripe Dashboard > API keys. Use environment variables.

name: Stripe Publishable Key (Live)
description: Stripe live publishable key in source code. While less sensitive than secret keys, it should still be managed carefully.
recommendation: Use environment variables to manage keys. Avoid committing to version control.

name: Generic API Key Assignment
description: Hardcoded API key assignment detected.
recommendation: Move API keys to environment variables or a secure vault. Never hardcode secrets.

name: Hardcoded Password
description: Hardcoded password or secret value detected in source code.
recommendation: Remove hardcoded passwords. Use environment variables, a secrets manager, or the vault.

name: Private Key
description: Private key detected in source code. Private keys must never be stored in code.
recommendation: Remove the private key immediately. Store it in a secure location. Generate new keys if compromised.

name: JWT Token
description: JSON Web Token detected. JWTs may contain sensitive claims and session data.
recommendation: Do not hardcode JWT tokens. Generate them at runtime and store securely.

name: Dynamic eval()
description: eval() called with dynamic input. This is a code injection vulnerability.
recommendation: Avoid eval() entirely. Use JSON.parse() for data, or Function constructor with strict validation if absolutely necessary.

name: Function Constructor with String
description: Function constructor used with string argument. This is equivalent to eval() and can execute arbitrary code.
recommendation: Avoid the Function constructor. Refactor to use direct function definitions or safe alternatives.

name: Command Injection Risk
description: Shell command execution with potentially user-controlled input. Command injection vulnerability.
recommendation: Use parameterized commands. Pass arguments as arrays to spawn(). Never interpolate user input into shell commands.

name: Data Exfiltration via Fetch
description: Network request near sensitive data access. Potential data exfiltration attempt.
recommendation: Review this code carefully. Ensure sensitive data is not being sent to external services.

name: Env Variable + Network Access
description: Environment variable access near outbound network call. Could indicate secret exfiltration.
recommendation: Verify that environment variables are not being sent to unauthorized endpoints.

name: Suspicious Base64 String
description: Long base64-encoded string detected. May contain obfuscated code or hidden data.
recommendation: Decode the string and inspect its contents. If it is code, refactor to use plain text.

name: Suspicious Hex-Encoded String
description: Long hex-encoded string detected. May be obfuscating malicious code.
recommendation: Decode and inspect the hex string. Replace with readable code if it is legitimate.

name: Character Code Obfuscation
description: String constructed from character codes. Common obfuscation technique in malicious code.
recommendation: Decode the character codes and inspect the resulting string. Replace with plain text.

name: Buffer with Encoding
description: Buffer.from() with explicit encoding. Legitimate in many cases but can be used for obfuscation.
recommendation: Verify the encoded data is legitimate and document its purpose.

name: Sensitive File Access
description: Reading from a sensitive system file or directory. May indicate credential theft.
recommendation: Verify this file access is necessary and authorized. Use the vault for credential storage instead.

name: Document Cookie Access
description: Accessing browser cookies. Ensure cookies are not being exfiltrated.
recommendation: Use HttpOnly cookies where possible. Avoid reading cookies in client-side JavaScript unless necessary.

name: localStorage with Sensitive Keys
description: Storing or reading sensitive data in localStorage. localStorage is not encrypted and accessible to any script.
recommendation: Use secure, HttpOnly cookies for session tokens. Use the vault for sensitive data storage.

name: WebSocket to Non-Standard Port
description: WebSocket connection to a non-standard port. May indicate a covert communication channel.
recommendation: Verify the WebSocket endpoint is legitimate and necessary.

name: Slack Webhook URL
description: Slack webhook URL detected. Can be used to post messages to Slack channels.
recommendation: Remove from source code. Store in environment variables or secrets manager.
